steps.title: Spot the parenthetical structure
steps.content: The phrase “a soft kind of rock” is set off by dashes and placed immediately after “steady like a rock.” This structure—placing a phrase right after another phrase to clarify it—indicates that the new phrase refines or qualifies what came before.

steps.title: Match to the correct answer
steps.content:
The phrase qualifies the description of steadiness—Mr. Verloc is characterized as steady in a soft, yielding way, not a rigid way.

The other three choices misread the function: none involve internal struggle, environmental contrast, or revealing a personal belief.

That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
